content.claim: Hungary ranks highest in the EU for school segregation of advantaged and disadvantaged students, with a socioeconomic achievement gap of 53 percentage points (compared to EU average of 33 points), and the highest segregation of Roma students in the region at 62% of Roma children attending predominantly Roma schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: EU Monitor and FRA data are recent but cross-sectional snapshots
Segregation measures do not isolate the causal contribution of early tracking versus residential patterns or historical inequality
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
